cdp.name: CDP
cdp.gloss: Customer Data Platform — the unified store of who your customers are.

perso.name: Personalization (1:1)
perso.gloss: Tailoring the message to the individual, not the segment.

genai.name: Generative AI
genai.gloss: Models that produce content — copy, images, replies — from a prompt.

agentic.name: Agentic AI
agentic.gloss: Systems that don't just generate, they decide and act toward a goal.

decis.name: Agentic decisioning
decis.gloss: An agent choosing what to say, when, how often and on which channel — per person.

bandit.name: Bandits / RL
bandit.gloss: The maths that lets a system learn which choice wins by trying and measuring.

cep.name: CEP
cep.gloss: Customer Engagement Platform — where this messaging actually runs.

geo.name: GEO
geo.gloss: Generative Engine Optimization — being visible inside AI answers.

govern.name: Governance
govern.gloss: The controls that make automated decisions safe, explainable and legible.

stories.head: Why Your Marketing Funnel Is Lying and What to Do About It
stories.plain: This opinion piece argues the classic linear marketing funnel no longer reflects how customers actually move toward a purchase. It challenges marketers to rethink dashboards and campaign structures built on that model. Relevant to AI-era marketing because non-linear customer journeys are a core reason teams are adopting agentic decisioning and CDPs to replace funnel-stage logic.

stories.head: Context — not the model — now decides what your AI marketing stack costs and whether it works
stories.plain: Research now shows the AI model itself is a commodity; what separates ROI from waste is the data and real-time signals wrapped around it — including a machine-readable brand layer that AI systems read when recommending. This reframes every buying decision in the martech stack away from model selection toward data infrastructure and context management.

stories.head: Gartner: customers 3× more likely to resolve issues via ChatGPT than a brand's own chatbot — AI investments chasing wrong returns
stories.plain: Gartner data reveals the function spending most on AI is showing the least return, and customers are bypassing brand-owned chatbots in favour of third-party AI tools. This is a direct challenge to current CX AI investment strategies and signals that deployment architecture — not budget size — determines outcomes.

stories.head: Only 16% of AI responses cite a brand — and citation rate hits zero in early B2B buyer stages
stories.plain: Demand-Genius research shows AI visibility metrics based on brand mentions are misleading: citations collapse to zero during awareness and consideration in B2B journeys. Marketers need to rethink how they measure and optimise presence inside AI-generated answers, making GEO strategy urgent rather than optional.

stories.head: Pega's Tara DeZao: Agentic AI shifts marketing from discrete tools to autonomous end-to-end orchestration
stories.plain: This practitioner interview with Pega's head of product marketing explains how agentic AI moves beyond content generation to managing full marketing workflows autonomously — understanding intent, sequencing tasks, and giving strategic feedback. It's a concrete guide to what agentic decisioning looks like in a real enterprise stack.

stories.head: Omneky launches public API and MCP server so any AI agent or platform can call its ad-creative engine
stories.plain: Omneky has opened its autonomous creative-generation engine via a Model Context Protocol server, letting developers, commerce platforms, and AI agents request on-brand ad creative programmatically. This is a structural change in how ad creative is built and bought — moving production into the agent layer rather than a human-operated UI.

month.skicker: 30-day synthesis · the shape underneath the feed

themes.head: Agentic AI Replaces the Prompt Era
body: Marketing AI is moving from single-shot prompts to autonomous agents that orchestrate multi-step workflows without human hand-holding at each stage. Platforms like Pega are framing this as a fundamental shift in how campaigns are planned, executed, and optimised — the marketer's job becomes setting goals and guardrails, not writing instructions.

The commercial proof is arriving fast: Omneky's public API & MCP server lets any downstream tool or agent trigger ad creative generation autonomously, and Cresta now uses AI agents to train human agents in real time. The architecture of the stack is changing — from tools you operate to agents you supervise.
ev: Agile Brand · Agentic orchestration with Pega
MarTech Series · Omneky autonomous creative API

themes.head: AI Search Rewrites Brand Visibility Rules
body: When AI assistants answer queries directly, the page-rank logic that powered a decade of SEO collapses. Brands are no longer competing for clicks — they are competing to be cited or recommended inside a conversational response, which means trust signals and authoritative context matter more than keyword density.

Research on how AI conversational context shapes brand recommendations shows that the framing of a question can exclude a brand entirely, even if it ranks well in traditional search. Marketers need to rethink measurement: impressions, clicks, and rankings miss the new visibility layer entirely.
ev: MarTech · Trust as visibility signal in AI search
Agile Brand · AI context shapes brand recommendations
MarTech · AI discovery changes what marketers measure

themes.head: The Context Cost Problem in AI Stacks
body: A quiet but consequential insight is emerging: the amount of context fed to an AI model determines both the quality of output and the size of the bill. Poorly scoped context windows inflate token costs and degrade results — making context management a core infrastructure decision, not just a prompt-engineering tip.

This reframes stack evaluation entirely. Choosing a CDP, a CMS, or a data platform now requires asking how well it packages and compresses context before handing it to a model. Teams that ignore this will face runaway inference costs and inconsistent outputs as they scale.
ev: Agile Brand · Context decides stack cost & performance
MarTech · CMS as AI operating system
MarTech · Scaling vibe coding beyond prompts

themes.head: Measurement & Funnels Are Structurally Broken
body: Multiple signals this month point to the same diagnosis: the linear funnel model actively misleads budget decisions. Rising customer acquisition costs without improving deal quality, and CAC climbing while conversion holds flat, suggest that optimising inside a broken model just accelerates the waste.

The problem is partly that AI-powered measurement tools are being applied to the wrong GTM motions — scaling volume at the top of a funnel that leaks badly in the middle. The corrective is redesigning measurement around buyer intent signals and revenue outcomes rather than funnel-stage proxies.
ev: Agile Brand · Marketing funnel is lying
MarTech · AI scaling the wrong part of GTM

themes.head: Who Builds Marketer Judgment When AI Does the Work?
body: With AI adoption reported at 90% across CX teams, a talent paradox is sharpening: junior marketers are being bypassed by automation before they can accumulate the pattern-recognition that makes senior judgment possible. The craft knowledge embedded in doing repetitive work — writing copy, segmenting audiences, analysing reports — is no longer being transferred.

The industry has no settled answer yet. Cresta's AI-trains-human-agents model is one experiment; others are debating whether structured apprenticeship programmes need to be rebuilt from scratch around AI-augmented workflows. How organisations solve this will determine whether marketing expertise compounds or hollows out over the next decade.
ev: MarTech · AI adoption hits 90% in CX
Agile Brand · Cresta AI training simulator

month.takeaway: Across every layer of the stack this month, the common thread is that the rules governing cost, visibility, measurement, and talent were all written for a pre-agentic world and need to be rewritten now.
